name: glass-dock
title: Glass Dock
description: A macOS-style liquid-glass application dock built on the refraction engine: an always-glass bar with crisp icons on top. The selected item rests as a soft pill; grab that pill and drag along the bar and it lifts into a glass lens that slides between items, snapping to the nearest one (and back to a pill) on release. Only the pill is draggable; pressing another item selects it.
docs: Import { GlassDock } from "@/components/ui/glass-dock". Built on the same `reveal`-mode Glass pattern as glass-tabs: the bar and the selection lens are two always-mounted glass markers over a uniform body (so there is no rectangular box artifact, and the glass never blanks during a drag). The icons/labels are a crisp layer on top. Pass `items` as an array of { id, label, icon, badge? }; control selection with value / defaultValue / onValueChange; optionally pass screen content as children to sit behind the bar. The selected item rests as a soft grey pill; pressing the pill and dragging lifts it into a glass lens that follows the pointer (only the pill is draggable - pressing another item just selects it), then snaps to the nearest item and settles back into a pill on release. The drag uses pointer capture so the grab cursor holds, and native image-drag is disabled. Tune the material with the optional `glass` prop (a LiquidGlassConfig: mode, specular, blur, refraction { amount, thickness }, translucency, shadow); DEFAULT_LIQUID_GLASS is exported.
